Title: Fertilization controls tiller numbers via MAX1 transcriptional regulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts carlactone (CL) into carlactonoic acid (CLA) during strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytohormones that inhibit tillering/branching and contribute to nutrient-deficiency responses and arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of Os1900 contributes to reduced CL-to-CLA conversion, CL accumulation, and increased tiller number; promoter downregulation can increase tillers and grain yield under low-fertilizer conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os1900 was identified from field transcriptome analysis as a fertilizer-responsive MAX1-like gene whose expression is repressed by fertilization. 2. Genetic analysis showed Os1900 acts together with Os5100 to control CL-to-CLA conversion in rice strigolactone biosynthesis. 3. os1900 single mutants did not increase tillers under normal fertilization, but showed mild tiller increase under trace fertilization. 4. Os1900 promoter deletion alleles altered fertilizer responsiveness; one allele (M4) lowered Os1900 expression and increased tiller number and grain yield under minor fertilizer conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for fertilizer-responsive tiller regulation; strict rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Field transcriptome analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; promoter deletion mutagenesis; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; (+)-GR24 rescue assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native Os1900 promoter; engineered Os1900 promoter deletion alleles (cis-regulatory editing)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in shoots including stem base and tiller base; transcripts detected around axillary bud apex regions; induced as fertilizer concentration declines and repressed rapidly after fertilization; reported as responsive to phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf expression is strongly repressed under moderate fertilization and rises under low soluble fertilizer conditions; expression at tiller base/shoot tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field fertilization regime; minor/trace/no fertilization; phosphate starvation in hydroponics for SL measurement; (+)-GR24 treatment; different photoperiod and temperature conditions for tillering assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single coding knockout has little effect under normal fertilization; some promoter alleles showed weak correlation between expression and tillering, and not all increased yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime target for cis-regulatory editing to create low-fertilizer-adapted rice; promoter mutations can tune expression without full loss of function, with M4 highlighted as useful for increasing tillers and grain yield under minor fertilizer input
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Field transcriptome microarray; RNA-seq (NCBI PRJNA889215); LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Os1900 alone has modest effect, but in combination with Os5100 is genetically decisive for CL-to-CLA conversion and tiller control; transcriptional regulation by fertilization is central to its biological role

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with Os1900 in conversion of carlactone (CL) to carlactonoic acid (CLA) during strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytohormones that inhibit tillering/branching and contribute to nutrient-deficiency responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Os5100 alone has little tiller phenotype under tested conditions, but loss together with Os1900 causes strong CL accumulation and increased tillering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os5100 is the closest homolog of Os1900 and was tested genetically with it. 2. os5100 single mutants did not significantly alter tiller number under normal fertilization. 3. os1900 os5100 double mutants strongly accumulated CL, showed very low CLA, and produced more tillers. 4. Os5100 is therefore a cooperative MAX1-like enzyme required with Os1900 for proper CL-to-CLA conversion in rice.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Contributory cooperative control point with Os1900; independent key-step status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; k-mer off-target analysis; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in shoot, stem base, tiller base, and around axillary bud apex regions; expression pattern slightly distinct from Os1900 near vascular tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in shoots and roots; reduced transcript abundance in double mutants due to gene disruption
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal/minor/trace fertilization conditions; phosphate starvation for SL analysis; different photoperiod and temperature conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single mutation did not noticeably improve tillering under normal fertilizer conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with Os1900 for manipulating strigolactone flux and tillering, though single-gene manipulation appears less useful than Os1900 promoter editing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike Os1900, fertilization-responsive transcriptional regulation was not emphasized for Os5100; its importance is mainly revealed genetically in the double mutant
